(redirected from Version0.Requires ) An accessor that requires a module that is not one of the Built-In JavaScript Modules should include a statement like var foo = require('@accessors-modules/module-name'); at the top-level of the specification. Thi

name'); at the top-level of the specification. This indicates to the accessor host that must have a locally installed implementation of the module in order to support this accessor. The host should reject the accessor if it does not have such an impl

ject the accessor if it does not have such an implementation installed. If a module defines a function called moduleFunction , then the script can invoke that function as follows: var module = require('@accessors-modules/module-name'); ... modu

('@accessors-modules/module-name'); ... module.moduleFunction(); Modules should be defined according to the Module Specification . TO DO: We need a module registry to avoid naming conflicts. Hosts should provide utilities like npm for installi
